footer.tagline: The complete guide to independent Australia. Ten atlases, one map.

home.heroSubtitle: The curated guide to Australia's best independent places — every one verified, mapped, and independently run.
home.searchHelper: Search a name — or just ask, in plain English. Describe what you're after (“a gift that fits in a suitcase”) and the Atlas answers with real places.
home.heroMapAria: Open the full interactive map of Australia — every verified place, mapped
home.spectrumNavAria: Browse the ten kinds by colour
home.worthFindingIntro: A few of the independent places we think you should know about — one cover story, and a handful more.
home.learnsYourTaste: An atlas that learns your taste
home.discoverIntro: Flick through independent Australia one place at a time. Keep the ones that catch your eye — every pick quietly tunes what the Atlas shows you next.
home.roadTripIntro: You know where you're going. We'll find the stops — the makers, the lunch, the detour worth taking.
home.planAStayTitle: Tell us the kind of trip. We'll build the days.
home.planAStayIntro: Coffee to start, a long lunch in the middle, and somewhere good to stay — a day-by-day trip drawn entirely from the independent places listed across the network.
home.clusterIntro: Regions where makers, stays, culture, and food overlap. One place, many reasons to go.
home.whatsOnIntro: Festivals, markets, and long-table dinners across the network.
home.noEventsTitle: Nothing on the calendar just yet.
home.noEventsIntro: Markets, festivals, openings, and long-table dinners will appear here as they're announced.
home.newsletterIntro: New openings, the occasional essay, and the quiet finds worth a detour — one considered email a week. No noise, no algorithms.
home.kindsOfPlaceIntro: Every place we list belongs to one of {word} categories. Browse a category on its own, or search across all of them at once.
home.phOpener: Find a roaster, a winery, a gallery, a farm stay…
home.phWine: Try “natural wine in the Adelaide Hills”
home.phGift: Or just ask: “a gift for my niece that's made in Australia”
home.phBirthday: Or ask: “somewhere to take mum for her birthday”
home.mapSectionAria: Explore the full interactive map of Australia
home.mapSectionAlt: A map of Australia with every verified independent place in the Atlas network marked as a coloured dot — clustered densely around the capital cities and coastlines, scattered through the interior.

trails.heroTitle: Plan a trip in plain English
trails.heroSubtitle: Describe the trip you want — a region, a theme, a duration — and we'll build a day-by-day itinerary from real, verified venues across the Atlas network. Or build your own trail from scratch.
trails.promptPlaceholder: Try 'weekend wine trail through the Barossa' or 'three day art tour of Hobart'...
trails.clickExample: Click any example to generate it instantly
trails.step1Desc: Tell us where, when, and what you love — wineries, makers, nature, coffee, or all of the above.
trails.step2Desc: Our engine pulls from thousands of verified listings across the Atlas network to create a day-by-day plan.
trails.step3Desc: Add stops, swap venues, save to your account, and share with friends.
trails.whatToKnow: What to know before you go
trails.shareBlurb: Send the link to a friend or save it for later.
trails.buildYourOwnLine1: Plan a custom route across Australia's best independent places.

explore.picksEmpty: No producer picks yet. As venues across the network vouch for one another, their picks will appear here.
explore.networkTitle: A living atlas of independent Australia
explore.networkSubtitle: Every number on this page is live. The network grows daily as venues are verified, claimed, and added across {countWord} curated directories.
explore.regionalCoverageDesc: Regions sorted by listing density. Lighter counts show where the network is still growing.
explore.missedTitle: Know a place we have missed?
explore.missedDesc: The Atlas grows best through word of mouth. If you know an independent venue, maker, or natural place that should be on the network, tell us.
explore.indepSubtitle: What it means to be on Australian Atlas.
explore.indepPledge: Every place on Australian Atlas is independently owned and operated. No chains. No franchises. No corporate groups. Just people running places they believe in.
explore.indepCriteria1Desc: The people behind the business are involved in running it day to day. Decisions are made by the people doing the work, not a board in another city.
explore.indepCriteria2Title: Not part of a national or international chain
explore.indepCriteria2Desc: No multi-site corporate brands. If it has hundreds of locations and a franchise model, it doesn’t belong here.
explore.indepCriteria3Desc: The menu, the stock, the opening hours, the character of the place — these are chosen by the operator, not dictated by a head office.
explore.indepCriteria4Desc: The best independent places are shaped by where they are. They source locally, respond to their community, and contribute to the character of a town or region.
explore.indepWhyPara1: Independent businesses are the difference between a town that feels like somewhere and one that could be anywhere. They create the texture of a place: the bakery that opens at five, the bookshop that stocks local authors, the gallery in the old post office. When you spend money at an independent business, more of it stays in the community. The owner lives nearby, hires locally, and reinvests in the street they trade on.
explore.indepWhyPara2: Australia has always been shaped by people who start things in the places they love. A cellar door in the Adelaide Hills. A ceramics studio on the mid-north coast. A coffee roaster in a Hobart laneway. These aren’t just businesses — they’re expressions of skill, taste, and commitment to a place. They make regional Australia worth the drive.
explore.indepWhyPara3: But independent places are harder to find. They don’t have the marketing budgets of chains. They don’t appear at the top of algorithm-driven platforms. Australian Atlas exists to change that — to make the independent layer of this country visible, searchable, and easy to support.
explore.indepPullQuote: The best places in Australia are the ones somebody put their name to.
explore.indepVerifyIntro: Keeping the network accurate is an ongoing process, not a one-time check. Here’s how we do it.
explore.indepVerify1Title: Every listing is reviewed before publishing
explore.indepVerify1Desc: New listings go through a verification process before they appear on the network. We check ownership structure, business registration, and chain affiliation.
explore.indepVerify2Desc: Visitors and locals can flag listings that no longer meet the criteria. If a place has been acquired by a chain or closed its doors, we hear about it quickly.
explore.indepVerify3Desc: Business owners can claim their listing for free to keep details current. Claimed listings are verified directly with the operator and updated in real time.
explore.indepVerify4Title: We remove places that no longer qualify
explore.indepVerify4Desc: If a business changes hands, joins a franchise, or no longer operates independently, we remove it from the network. No exceptions, no grandfathering.
explore.indepCtaTitle: Know an independent place that should be here?
explore.indepCtaDesc: If you know a place that’s independently owned, genuinely good, and deserves to be found — we want to hear about it.
explore.aboutHeroSubtitle: Mapping the small batch producers, the family-run stays, the bookshops, the galleries you stumble upon, and the places that make this country worth exploring slowly.
explore.aboutStory1: There is another Australia underneath the one the big platforms show you. It is the ceramics studio in a regional town, the distillery in a converted dairy, the swimming hole the locals know about, the vintage shop worth the detour. These places rarely appear on mainstream travel sites. They are too small, too independent, too particular.
explore.aboutStory2: Australian Atlas exists to map that layer. Ten curated atlases cover every dimension of independent culture across {regions} regions. Each place is checked, categorised, and maintained — a reference work, not a scrape.
explore.aboutStory2WithListings: Australian Atlas exists to map that layer. Ten curated atlases cover every dimension of independent culture across {regions} regions and {listings} verified listings. Each place is checked, categorised, and maintained — a reference work, not a scrape.
explore.aboutStory3: I started this because I kept finding places that deserved more visibility than an Instagram post or a Google pin. A distiller doing something genuinely interesting in a shed outside Castlemaine. A bookshop in a town of 400 people. These are the places that make a region worth the drive, and nobody was mapping them properly.
explore.belief1Body: No chains. No franchises. No paid placements. Every listing in the network exists because someone built something real, not because they bought visibility. If a place is here, it is because it is independently run and worth knowing about.
explore.belief2Body: Every listing is verified, geocoded, and categorised. We check URLs, phone numbers, and business status. Venues that cannot be verified are excluded. We would rather have a smaller, accurate atlas than a large, unreliable one.
explore.belief3Body: Australia has an extraordinary independent layer: the makers, the growers, the people running galleries in converted woolsheds and roasting coffee in country towns. Most platforms miss it. We exist to map it.
explore.belief4Body: Australian Atlas is built and maintained by one person in Australia. It is not a venture-backed startup chasing growth metrics. It is a reference work, built with care, and intended to last.
explore.aboutNetworkSubtitle: Each atlas covers a distinct category of independent place. Together, they form the most comprehensive guide to independent Australia.
explore.atlas_sba_desc: Craft drink. The distillers, brewers, winemakers, and cidermakers doing it independently.
explore.atlas_craft_desc: Makers. Ceramicists, woodworkers, textile artists, glassblowers, and studio potters.
explore.atlas_collection_desc: Museums, galleries, heritage spaces, and artist-run initiatives worth the visit.
explore.atlas_fine_grounds_desc: Specialty coffee. Single-origin roasters, independent cafes, and the places that take it seriously.
explore.atlas_rest_desc: Boutique stays. The cabin in the ranges, the guesthouse on the coast, the converted shed.
explore.atlas_field_desc: Natural places. Swimming holes, walking trails, lookouts, and the landscapes between towns.
explore.atlas_corner_desc: Independent shops. Bookshops, record stores, design studios, and retailers who stock with intent.
explore.atlas_found_desc: Vintage and secondhand. Op shops, antique dealers, salvage yards, and the thrill of the find.
explore.atlas_table_desc: Food producers. Farm gates, bakeries, providores, and the people growing and making what you eat.
explore.atlas_way_desc: Experiences. Guided walks, cultural tours, sailing charters, and the people who know the country.
explore.audTravellersBody: Discover independent places across the country. Build trails, save favourites, and plan trips around the things that actually make a region interesting.
explore.audOperatorsBody: If you run an independent venue, your listing may already be here. Claim it for free to update your details, or subscribe for enhanced features.
explore.audCouncilsBody: Tourism bodies and regional councils get access to verified data, regional dashboards, and embeddable content for their own platforms.
explore.aboutCommunityTitle: Know a place we should list?
explore.aboutCommunityDesc: The network gets better when people contribute. If you know an independent place that should be listed — a maker, a producer, a shop, a swimming hole — let me know.
explore.aboutPartOf: Australian Atlas is part of <link>Australian Heritage</link>, an editorial network focused on Australian culture, place, and identity.
explore.indexMetaTitle: Atlas Index — Every Independent Place in Australia
explore.indexMetaDescription: Browse all {count} independent Australian places alphabetically. The complete A-Z directory of the Australian Atlas network.
